1 Landing — Stack First Look
interest Confidence 0.50

"Alright, ShipFast. 'Ship your startup in days, not weeks' -- heard that pitch before. Let me check the stack first... Next.js, Tailwind, Resend, Stripe/Lemon Squeezy, NextAuth, MongoDB/Supabase. The stack diagram is right there on the hero -- actually useful. But wait, MongoDB *and* Supabase? I need to know if I can pick one or if it's some weird hybrid setup."

✓ Hero stack diagram instantly confirms tech compatibility for indie hacker
2 Social Proof & Time Savings
interest Confidence 0.50

"A testimonial from Jack Friks -- $5,868 MRR, decent for a side project. Featured on Hacker News, Product Hunt, Reddit... social proof checks out. This time breakdown -- 4 hours for emails, 6 for landing page, 4 for Stripe webhooks... yeah, I literally spent 3 weeks on exactly this stuff for my last project. But this is marketing fluff. I want to see actual code structure."

✓ Time breakdown resonates — matches exact 3-week boilerplate pain from last project
3 Feature Tabs — Database
interest Confidence 0.55

"'22+ hours of headaches, plus infinity hours overthinking' -- ha, okay Marc, I feel personally attacked. That `const launch_time` code snippet is a nice developer marketing touch. 4 Database Tab — Too Shallow
interest Confidence 0.50

"Mongoose schema OR Postgres tables. MongoDB with Mongoose, or Supabase with Postgres. 'ShipFast is modular -- you can easily swap.' Good, I use Supabase. But... 'Mongoose plugins to make your life easier' -- that bullet only mentions Mongoose. Is the Supabase integration equally fleshed out? And only 3 bullet points for 'Time saved: 2 hours'? I'd like to see the actual schema, migration setup, whether they use Prisma or raw SQL... This is way too surface-level."

⚠ Database section has 3 shallow bullets — Supabase integration quality unclear
5 Founder Story — Credibility
interest Confidence 0.60

"Marc Lou -- Maker of the Year 2023, shipped 16 startups in 2 years, $45k/month. Actually impressive. The demo video thumbnail shows a file tree with mailgun, stripe, dashboard, privacy-policy, tos... and JS/TS badges. Wait, is this JavaScript or TypeScript? It says both. I need to know if the codebase is fully typed. Let me check pricing first -- I want to compare tiers before going deeper."

✓ Founder credentials and real MRR data are highly credible to indie hacker audience
6 Pricing — Three Tiers
interest Confidence 0.65

"Three tiers -- Starter $199, All-in $249, Bundle with CodeFast $299. The $100 off '13 left' is obviously fake scarcity -- classic indie hacker marketing trick. Let me compare... Starter has NextJS boilerplate, SEO & Blog, Mailgun emails, Stripe/Lemon Squeezy, MongoDB/Supabase, Google OAuth & Magic Links, Components & animations, ChatGPT prompts. But Discord community is crossed out. The $50 difference between Starter and All-in is small enough that I'd probably go All-in."

⚠ Fake scarcity counter is transparent to indie hackers — minor trust erosion
7 Full Tier Comparison
trust Confidence 0.70

"So the difference: Starter misses Discord community, $1,210 discounts, and -- wait -- Lifetime updates? Starter doesn't get lifetime updates? If the codebase gets updated and I'm stuck on old version, that's a problem. For $50 more I get lifetime updates... no-brainer for All-in. 'Updated 2 months ago' -- good, it's maintained. 'Pay once. Build unlimited projects!' -- no subscription, important for indie hackers. CodeFast bundle is a learning course, not what I need. All-in at $249 is the clear choice."

⚠ 'No lifetime updates' on Starter is the key diff but visually buried in the list
8 FAQ — TypeScript Confirmation
interest Confidence 0.72

"'Javascript or Typescript?' -- 'Both! You can choose once you get access.' So they maintain two versions. I'd pick TypeScript obviously. But 'you can choose once you get access' -- meaning I can't preview the TS codebase before buying? That's a leap of faith. '/app router or /pages router?' -- same answer, 'Both!' So 4 combinations? Impressive if true, but the answers are so terse."

⚠ Cannot preview TS codebase quality before purchase — high leap of faith for $249
9 FAQ — App Router Answer
annoyance Confidence 0.70

"Same one-liner again. 'Both! You can choose once you get access.' Is the app router version using Server Components properly? Server Actions? Same feature set across all 4 variants? These one-line FAQ answers are frustrating. For $249, I expect architectural details, not marketing one-liners."

⚠ One-line FAQ answers for technical architecture questions frustrate developer buyers
10 FAQ Summary — Decision
interest Confidence 0.75

"Better. Three things: 1) NextJS starter with payment, database, login, blog, UI components, in JS/TS and /app or /pages router. 2) Documentation to set up from scratch. 3) Premium plan gets Discord with 5,000+ makers. The Discord community is actually valuable -- real-world usage feedback. But still... I'm a code-quality person. I want to see file structure, naming conventions, error boundaries, TypeScript types quality. The landing page has zero actual code previews. I've evaluated enough to decide though."

✓ Enough information gathered — deciding to buy despite code preview gap
